sections.paragraph:
of both genders (m. Num 21:5 f. Gen 49:20).

(1) food, both of men and of beasts, Lev 3:11 Psa 41:10 102:5 Job 20:14 לֶחֶם אֱלֹהִים the food of God, used of sacrifice, Lev 21:8, 17 Lev 21:17. Jer 11:19, עֵץ בְּלַחְמֹו “the tree with its food,” i.e. its fruit (compare Arab. أُكْلُ food, used of fruit). לֶחֶם הַפֶּחָה the food of the governor, the provision for his table (Tafelgelder), Neh 5:18 comp. verse 15 לַחְמְךָ Oba 1:7, ellipt. for אַנְשֵׁי לַחְמְךָ those who eat of thy table, thy household; used of banquets, or meals, in the phrases אָכַל לֶחֶם to eat food (see אָכַל 1, let. ); and עָשָׂה לֶחֶם to furnish a banquet or meal, Ecc 10:19.

(2) specially bread (as in Arab. لَحْمُ is specially flesh); לֶחֶם הַפָּנִים bread of the presence; LXX. ἄρτοι ἐνώπιοι; Vulg. panes propositionis; Luth. Schaubrodte; [Engl. Trans. shew-bread.] Twelve small loaves which were set out in the holy tabernacle before Jehovah every week (in the manner of lectisternia), Exo 25:30 35:13 39:36 called in the later books לֶחֶם הַמַּעֲרֶכֶת. When numerals are prefixed, כִּכְּרֹות must be supplied; 1Sa 10:4, שְׁתֵּי לֶחֶם two (loaves) of bread; compare verse 1Sa 10:3, once even-

(3) wheat, of the flour of which the Hebrews made their bread; bread-corn, Brotkorn (comp. Arab. طَعَامُ food; specially wheat; and on the other hand, Gr. σῖτος, wheat, then food of any kind). Isa 28:28, לֶחֶם יוּדַק וְגו׳ “wheat is threshed indeed, but they do not beat it hard,” etc.
